adds.rationale: Rocket Lab's November 2022 Q3 earnings confirmed $63.1M in record quarterly revenue (14% QoQ, 1,093% YoY) — a concrete commercial revenue inflection — placing the company squarely in the buildup phase of the profile's first-named 'next wave,' and no rockets/spacecraft name is currently in the watchlist.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab Q3 FY2022 press release (Form 8-K): record revenue of $63.1M, +14% quarter-over-quarter and +1,093% year-over-year; Q4 guidance set at $51M-$54M as one launch customer shifted into 2023.
news_evidence.source: SEC EDGAR — Rocket Lab USA Form 8-K FY2022

news_evidence.summary: Yahoo Finance reports RKLB stock rose sharply on Q3 2022 earnings beat, citing growing commercial launch manifest and expanding space systems (satellite components) revenue alongside launch services.

adds.rationale: Russia's ongoing Ukraine war triggered durable structural rearmament commitments across NATO — NATO members raised equipment-spending share to 26% of defense budgets in 2022 (from 14% in 2014) — and ITA returned +9.95% in 2022 while the S&P 500 fell roughly 18%, confirming the wave is already in motion with multi-year procurement cycles ahead.

news_evidence.summary: iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA) posted a 2022 calendar-year market-price return of +9.95%, sharply outperforming the broad S&P 500 decline; ETF launched May 2006 and tracks US prime contractors including LMT, RTX, and NOC.

Atlantic Council NATO defense spending tracker: NATO allies raised the average share of defense budgets devoted to equipment from 14% in 2014 to 26% in 2022 following Russia's invasion of Ukraine, with member states pledging to meet or exceed the 2% GDP spending target.

adds.rationale: Constellation Energy, the largest US nuclear fleet operator (spun off from Exelon in February 2022), reported solid Q3 2022 Adjusted EBITDA of $592M and its CEO explicitly cited the August 2022 Inflation Reduction Act as a structural tailwind for nuclear economics; the company also filed NRC intent to renew operating licenses at Clinton and Dresden, locking in decade-plus baseload generation that aligns directly with the profile's AI-data-center nuclear thesis.

news_evidence.summary: Constellation Energy Q3 2022 results: Adjusted EBITDA $592M; CEO Joe Dominguez cited the Inflation Reduction Act as enabling Constellation to 'create value and drive America's clean energy transition'; company notified NRC of intent to seek license renewals for Clinton and Dresden nuclear units.
Constellation Energy Corp Form 8-K (Q2 FY2022): detailed nuclear fleet operational results and financial outlook confirming consistent performance across the company's 21-unit nuclear fleet.
news_evidence.source: SEC EDGAR — Constellation Energy Form 8-K FY2022

rationale_overall: The portfolio enters this rebalance as a heavily AI-concentrated bundle of four megacap tech names plus SPY, with zero exposure to any of the profile's seven named 'next waves.' All four AI holdings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) retain their thesis — data-center demand is intact, no fundamental breach occurred — so no removes are warranted despite the broad 2022 tech selloff. The three adds fill all open slots to reach the max_watchlist_size of 8: RKLB opens the rockets/spacecraft lane at a commercially meaningful moment (record quarterly revenue, established launch manifest); ITA opens the defense/geopolitical lane as NATO rearmament transitions from rhetorical pledges to actual equipment procurement budgets; and CEG opens the nuclear lane at precisely the moment the Inflation Reduction Act improved nuclear unit economics and Constellation committed to decade-plus license renewals. The net effect is to dilute the AI concentration from 80% of ticker slots to 50%, while adding three thematically distinct exposures that each have durable, multi-year structural tailwinds named explicitly in the investor profile.
